project.name: UltraDAG
project.description: Minimal DAG-BFT blockchain in Rust with sub-4MB binary, 2-3 round deterministic finality, passkey-native wallets, runs on a $15 Raspberry Pi. Built for IoT and machine-to-machine micropayments.

nodes.name: checkpoint.rs
nodes.summary: Implements checkpoint creation, signing, verification, and deterministic state root computation for periodic consensus snapshots that enable fast sync and state validation.
nodes.languageNotes: compute_state_root is a 440-line function that deterministically hashes the entire state snapshot including accounts, stakes, delegations, proposals, votes, council members, and governance params.

nodes.name: Checkpoint
nodes.summary: Represents a finalized checkpoint with round, state root, DAG tip, total supply, and multi-validator signatures for BFT quorum acceptance.

nodes.name: compute_state_root
nodes.summary: Computes a deterministic SHA-256 state root over the entire state snapshot including accounts, stakes, delegations, proposals, votes, council members, and governance parameters for checkpoint verification.

nodes.name: dag.rs
nodes.summary: Core BlockDag data structure implementing the directed acyclic graph for DAG-BFT consensus, including vertex insertion with validation, parent selection, equivocation detection, descendant-validator BitVec tracking, pruning, and persistence.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses BitVec for descendant_validators providing O(1) finality checks with 256x memory reduction over HashSet. ValidatorIndex provides bidirectional Address-to-usize mapping for bitmap indexing.

nodes.name: BlockDag
nodes.summary: The primary DAG structure storing vertices, parent-child relationships, tips, round indices, equivocation evidence, and descendant validator bitmaps for BFT finality determination.

nodes.name: ValidatorIndex
nodes.summary: Bidirectional mapping between validator addresses and compact usize indices, enabling efficient BitVec-based descendant tracking.

nodes.name: try_insert
nodes.summary: Validates and inserts a vertex into the DAG with signature verification, timestamp checks, parent existence validation, round constraints, equivocation detection, and MAX_PARENTS enforcement.

nodes.name: prune_old_rounds_with_depth
nodes.summary: Prunes DAG vertices older than the specified depth below the last finalized round, removing vertices, children, tips, round indices, descendant bitmaps, and validator mappings to bound memory usage.

nodes.name: do_insert
nodes.summary: Low-level vertex insertion that updates vertices map, children map, tips set, round index, validator-round index, and propagates descendant validator bitmaps up the DAG.

nodes.name: verify_integrity
nodes.summary: Validates internal DAG consistency by checking parent-child symmetry, tip correctness, round index accuracy, and vertex-round mapping integrity.

nodes.name: select_parents
nodes.summary: Selects up to k parent vertices from the DAG tips for a new vertex, preferring tips from different validators and excluding the proposer's own tips.

nodes.name: find_newly_finalized
nodes.summary: Scans the DAG for vertices that have achieved 2f+1 descendant validator support and returns newly finalized vertices in topological order.

nodes.name: epoch.rs
nodes.summary: Synchronizes the validator set for each epoch by reading staked validators from the state engine and updating the finality tracker's allowed set and configured count.

nodes.name: sync_epoch_validators
nodes.summary: Reads the active validator set from state engine stakes and updates the finality tracker's allowed validators and configured count for the current epoch.

nodes.name: finality.rs
nodes.summary: Implements the FinalityTracker that determines when DAG vertices achieve BFT finality (2f+1 descendant validators), manages the finalized set, tracks rounds, and handles DAG pruning of finalized data.
nodes.languageNotes: find_newly_finalized treats pruned parents (dag.get(p).is_none()) as finalized and stuck parents >100 rounds behind as finalized for liveness.

nodes.name: FinalityTracker
nodes.summary: Tracks validator registrations, computes 2f+1 finality thresholds, determines newly finalized vertices by descendant validator counting, and manages the finalized set with persistence support.

nodes.name: find_newly_finalized
nodes.summary: Core finality algorithm: scans unfinalized vertices round-by-round, checks descendant validator count against 2f+1 threshold (using active set filtering), handles stuck parents and pruned-as-finalized semantics, and returns newly finalized hashes in topological order.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Module barrel file that re-exports all consensus submodules: checkpoint, dag, epoch, finality, ordering, persistence, validator_set, and vertex.

nodes.name: ordering.rs
nodes.summary: Defines deterministic topological ordering of finalized DAG vertices by (round, hash) for consistent transaction execution across all nodes. Contains only test functions validating ordering properties.

nodes.name: persistence.rs
nodes.summary: Defines DagSnapshot and FinalitySnapshot structs for serializing and deserializing the BlockDag and FinalityTracker state to/from JSON files.

nodes.name: DagSnapshot
nodes.summary: Serializable snapshot of BlockDag state including vertices, children, tips, rounds, byzantine validators, equivocation evidence, and pruning floor for persistence.

nodes.name: FinalitySnapshot
nodes.summary: Serializable snapshot of FinalityTracker state including finalized set, validators, min_validators, and last_finalized_round.

nodes.name: validator_set.rs
nodes.summary: Implements ValidatorSet with registration, allowlist-based filtering, BFT quorum threshold calculation (2f+1), and validator lifecycle management for consensus participation.

nodes.name: ValidatorSet
nodes.summary: Manages the active validator set with allowlist filtering, registration/removal, quorum threshold computation (2f+1 where f = floor((n-1)/3)), and iteration over active addresses.

nodes.name: vertex.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the DagVertex struct representing a single DAG node with block payload, parent hashes, round, validator identity, Ed25519 signature, and methods for hashing, signing, and timestamp validation.

nodes.name: DagVertex
nodes.summary: Core DAG vertex containing a block, parent hashes, round number, validator address, public key, Ed25519 signature, and topo_level, with methods for deterministic hashing and signature/timestamp verification.

nodes.name: bridge.rs
nodes.summary: Defines BridgeDepositTx and BridgeReleaseTx for cross-chain bridge operations, including signable byte construction, Ed25519 signature verification, and validator-federation deposit authorization.

nodes.name: BridgeDepositTx
nodes.summary: Transaction type for depositing UDAG into the bridge for cross-chain transfer, with destination chain ID, signature verification, and validator set authorization.

nodes.name: BridgeReleaseTx
nodes.summary: Transaction type for releasing bridged UDAG from a source chain deposit, linking back to the original deposit nonce for replay protection.

nodes.name: delegate.rs
nodes.summary: Defines DelegateTx, UndelegateTx, and SetCommissionTx for delegated staking operations, enabling token holders to delegate stake to validators and validators to set commission rates.

nodes.name: DelegateTx
nodes.summary: Transaction for delegating UDAG stake to a validator address, with Ed25519 signature verification and deterministic hashing.

nodes.name: UndelegateTx
nodes.summary: Transaction for initiating undelegation cooldown, withdrawing stake from a validator.

nodes.name: SetCommissionTx
nodes.summary: Transaction for validators to set their commission percentage on delegated rewards.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Barrel module that re-exports all transaction types, the mempool, smart account types, name registry types, streaming payment types, and staking constants from the tx submodules.

nodes.name: name_registry.rs
nodes.summary: Implements the on-chain name registry with tiered annual fees, name validation rules, pocket address derivation, and four transaction types: RegisterNameTx, RenewNameTx, TransferNameTx, and UpdateProfileTx for human-readable identity management.

nodes.name: validate_name
nodes.summary: Validates a name against length, character set (lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens), and structural rules (no leading/trailing hyphens).

nodes.name: validate_pocket_label
nodes.summary: Validates pocket sub-labels (e.g. 'savings' in '@alice.savings') against length and character constraints for multi-wallet identity pockets.

nodes.name: derive_pocket_address
nodes.summary: Deterministically derives a pocket address from a parent address and label using blake3 hashing, enabling sub-wallets without separate key management.

nodes.name: persistence.rs
nodes.summary: Provides MempoolSnapshot struct for serializing and deserializing the mempool to disk, delegating to the shared persistence module for JSON file I/O.

nodes.name: pool.rs
nodes.summary: Implements the in-memory transaction pool (Mempool) with per-sender caps, TTL-based eviction, fee-priority insertion with lowest-fee eviction when full, and fee-sorted batch selection for block production.

nodes.name: Mempool
nodes.summary: In-memory transaction pool with HashMap-based storage, per-sender secondary index, size limits (10K global, 100 per sender), TTL eviction, and fee-priority ordering for block production.

nodes.name: insert_with_reason
nodes.summary: Core mempool insertion logic that enforces per-sender caps, global size limits, and fee-priority eviction when the pool is full.

nodes.name: smart_account.rs
nodes.summary: Comprehensive smart account system supporting passkey-native (P256/WebAuthn) wallets with multi-key management, social recovery, spending policies, vault time-locks, sponsored transactions (FeePayer), and the generic SmartOpTx envelope that wraps staking/governance/naming/streaming operations for passkey signing.
nodes.languageNotes: Supports both Ed25519 and P256 (secp256r1) signature verification with DER and raw format parsing. SmartOpType enum uses 17 variants to unify all transaction types under a single passkey-signed envelope.

nodes.name: verify_p256
nodes.summary: Verifies a P256 (secp256r1) signature over a message, accepting both DER-encoded and raw (r||s 64-byte) formats with compressed or uncompressed public keys.

nodes.name: SmartAccountConfig
nodes.summary: Per-address smart account configuration storing authorized keys (max 10), social recovery config, spending policy, and pocket sub-wallets.

nodes.name: SmartOpType
nodes.summary: Enum with 17 variants representing all operations a passkey wallet can perform: staking, delegation, governance, name registry, streaming payments, key management, and pocket operations.

nodes.name: SmartOpTx
nodes.summary: Generic passkey-signed transaction envelope that wraps SmartOpType operations with WebAuthn authenticator data and client data JSON for P256 signature verification.

nodes.name: stake.rs
nodes.summary: Defines StakeTx and UnstakeTx for validator staking operations with a 2,000 UDAG minimum stake and 2,016-round cooldown period (~2.8 hours), including Ed25519 signature construction and verification.

nodes.name: StakeTx
nodes.summary: Transaction for staking UDAG to become a validator, with signable bytes prefixed by network ID and Ed25519 signature verification.

nodes.name: UnstakeTx
nodes.summary: Transaction for initiating unstake cooldown to withdraw validator stake after UNSTAKE_COOLDOWN_ROUNDS delay.

nodes.name: stream.rs
nodes.summary: Implements streaming payments with configurable rate-per-round, vesting cliff support, and three transaction types (CreateStreamTx, WithdrawStreamTx, CancelStreamTx) for continuous fund disbursement between addresses.

nodes.name: Stream
nodes.summary: Streaming payment state tracking sender, recipient, rate, cliff, deposited/withdrawn amounts, and cancellation status with helper methods for accrual calculation.

nodes.name: transaction.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the unified Transaction enum (27 variants) covering transfers, staking, delegation, governance, bridge, smart account, name registry, streaming, and SmartOp operations, plus the TransferTx struct with optional memo payload and dispatch methods for hash, verify, fee, nonce, and signable_bytes.
nodes.languageNotes: Variants must be appended at the end to preserve postcard serialization compatibility. The match-based dispatch across 27 variants is the core routing layer for all transaction processing.

nodes.name: Transaction
nodes.summary: Unified transaction enum with 27 variants dispatching hash, verify_signature, from, nonce, fee, amount, pub_key, signable_bytes, and total_cost across all transaction types via exhaustive match arms.

nodes.name: TransferTx
nodes.summary: Basic UDAG transfer transaction with sender, recipient, amount, fee, nonce, Ed25519 signature, and optional memo payload (max 256 bytes) for IoT data or receipts.

nodes.name: db.rs
nodes.summary: Serializes and deserializes the full StateEngine to/from redb (embedded key-value store), handling all tables including accounts, stakes, delegations, proposals, votes, council members, smart accounts, name registry, streams, and bridge state.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses redb table definitions with typed keys/values and postcard binary serialization for compact storage.

nodes.name: save_to_redb
nodes.summary: Persists the entire StateEngine to a redb database file, writing all state tables (accounts, stakes, delegations, governance, bridge, smart accounts, names, streams) in a single write transaction.

nodes.name: load_from_redb
nodes.summary: Reconstructs a full StateEngine from a redb database file by reading all persisted tables and rebuilding in-memory indexes including pocket_to_parent map.

nodes.name: engine.rs
nodes.summary: Core state machine of the UltraDAG blockchain containing the StateEngine struct with all account, staking, delegation, governance, bridge, smart account, name registry, and streaming payment logic plus ~30 integration tests.
nodes.languageNotes: Massive 6700-line file combining the entire blockchain state transition logic with inline tests. StateEngine holds ~30 HashMaps covering every subsystem.

nodes.name: StateEngine
nodes.summary: Central state machine holding all blockchain state: accounts, stakes, delegations, governance proposals/votes, council members, bridge attestations/signatures, smart accounts, name registry, streaming payments, and transaction index.

nodes.name: StakeAccount
nodes.summary: Tracks a validator's staked amount, commission rate, pending unstake details, and cooldown timing for the delegated proof-of-stake system.

nodes.name: DelegationAccount
nodes.summary: Represents a delegation from one address to a validator, tracking delegated amount and unlock timing.

nodes.name: apply_finalized_vertices
nodes.summary: Applies a batch of finalized DAG vertices to state, executing transactions, distributing per-round rewards, processing unstake completions, slashing equivocators, ticking governance, and verifying the supply invariant atomically.

nodes.name: tick_governance
nodes.summary: Processes all active governance proposals at round boundaries: evaluates quorum/threshold, executes passed proposals (parameter changes, treasury spends, council membership, emergency actions), and marks expired proposals as failed.

nodes.name: distribute_round_rewards
nodes.summary: Distributes per-round emission rewards to active producers, passive stakers, council members, and treasury according to the 7-bucket tokenomics model.

nodes.name: snapshot
nodes.summary: Creates a deterministic checkpoint snapshot of the full state engine for persistence and sync, sorting all collections for cross-node consistency.

nodes.name: apply_smart_op_tx
nodes.summary: Applies smart account operations (add/remove keys, set vault policy, execute vault, set recovery, create/cancel streams, register/transfer names, create/remove pockets) with multi-key authorization and per-operation validation.

nodes.name: slash_at_round
nodes.summary: Slashes a validator's stake by 50% for equivocation at a specific round, cascading to delegators and recording the event to prevent double-slashing.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Barrel module re-exporting the state subsystem: engine (StateEngine), persistence (StateSnapshot), and db (redb serialization).

nodes.name: persistence.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the JSON-serializable StateSnapshot struct for checkpoint sync, containing all state fields needed to reconstruct a StateEngine from a checkpoint file.

nodes.name: StateSnapshot
nodes.summary: Serializable snapshot of complete blockchain state used for checkpoint-based sync, including accounts, stakes, delegations, governance, bridge state, smart accounts, name registry, and streams.

nodes.name: council.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the Council of 21 seat categories (Engineering, Community, Growth, Operations, Legal, Research, Security) with max seat counts and serialization, plus the CouncilAction enum for add/remove membership proposals.

nodes.name: CouncilSeatCategory
nodes.summary: Enum defining the 7 council seat categories (Engineering, Community, Growth, Operations, Legal, Research, Security) with methods for max seats, listing, and name parsing.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Barrel module re-exporting all governance types: council categories/actions, governance parameters, proposal types/status, and governance transaction structs.

nodes.name: params.rs
nodes.summary: Defines GovernanceParams with ~20 runtime-adjustable protocol parameters (quorum, voting period, staking limits, fee rates, emission splits, bridge thresholds) and the apply_change method that validates and applies parameter change proposals.

nodes.name: GovernanceParams
nodes.summary: Struct holding all runtime-adjustable governance parameters including quorum percentages, voting periods, staking limits, fee rates, emission distribution splits, and bridge configuration thresholds.

nodes.name: apply_change
nodes.summary: Validates and applies a named parameter change to GovernanceParams, enforcing per-parameter bounds and constraints (e.g., emission percentages must sum to 100).

nodes.name: proposals.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the Proposal struct and ProposalType enum (ParameterChange, TreasurySpend, EmergencyAction, CouncilMembership, TextProposal) with voting logic and pass/fail evaluation using snapshot-based quorum.

nodes.name: ProposalType
nodes.summary: Enum representing the five types of governance proposals: parameter changes, treasury spends, emergency actions, council membership changes, and text-only proposals.

nodes.name: Proposal
nodes.summary: Governance proposal with proposer, type, status, vote counts, creation round, voting deadline, and snapshot_total_stake for manipulation-resistant quorum calculation.

nodes.name: transactions.rs
nodes.summary: Defines CreateProposalTx and VoteTx transaction types with signable byte construction, hashing, and Ed25519 signature verification for council-gated governance operations.

nodes.name: CreateProposalTx
nodes.summary: Transaction type for creating governance proposals, containing proposer address, proposal type, title, description, nonce, and signature with deterministic hash construction.

nodes.name: VoteTx
nodes.summary: Transaction type for casting a governance vote, containing voter address, proposal ID, vote direction, nonce, and signature.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Barrel module re-exporting the block producer's create_block function.

nodes.name: producer.rs
nodes.summary: Implements the create_block function that assembles a DAG vertex block from mempool transactions with byte-budget enforcement, sorting by sender/nonce for valid execution order, and trimming oversized blocks proportionally.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses postcard serialization to check block size against MAX_VERTEX_BYTES budget, trimming transactions proportionally rather than one-at-a-time for efficiency.

nodes.name: block.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the Block struct (header, coinbase, transactions) with hash computation, merkle root calculation, fee totaling, and test helper functions for block and transaction construction.

nodes.name: Block
nodes.summary: Core block structure containing a header, coinbase transaction, and list of user transactions.

nodes.name: merkle_root
nodes.summary: Computes a SHA-256 Merkle tree root from a list of leaf hashes, used for transaction commitment in block headers.

nodes.name: genesis.rs
nodes.summary: Constructs the genesis block with hardcoded parameters (height 0, zero prev_hash, genesis timestamp) and provides the GENESIS_CHECKPOINT_HASH constant for chain identity verification.

nodes.name: header.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the BlockHeader struct (version, height, timestamp, prev_hash, merkle_root) with SHA-256 hash computation.

nodes.name: BlockHeader
nodes.summary: Block header containing version, height, timestamp, previous block hash, and merkle root fields.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Barrel module re-exporting block types (Block, BlockHeader), genesis block construction, merkle root computation, and test helpers.

nodes.name: keys.rs
nodes.summary: Implements the Address (32-byte Ed25519 public key hash), SecretKey (Ed25519 signing), and Signature types with hex and bech32 encoding, providing the cryptographic identity foundation for all UltraDAG transactions.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ed25519-dalek for signing, sha2 for address derivation, and bech32 for human-readable addresses with 'udag1' prefix.

nodes.name: Address
nodes.summary: 32-byte address derived from SHA-256 hash of Ed25519 public key, supporting hex and bech32 ('udag1') encoding formats.

nodes.name: SecretKey
nodes.summary: Ed25519 signing key wrapper providing key generation, address derivation, and transaction signing.

nodes.name: Signature
nodes.summary: Ed25519 signature with verification methods supporting both Address-based and raw public key byte verification.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Barrel module re-exporting Address, SecretKey, and Signature types from the keys submodule.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Implements the validator federation bridge for cross-chain transfers to Arbitrum, including BridgeAttestation construction with Solidity-compatible hashing, secp256k1 signing derived from Ed25519 keys, BridgeProof assembly with 2/3 threshold verification, and comprehensive tests.
nodes.languageNotes: Maintains dual signing scheme: Ed25519 for consensus, secp256k1 for Solidity ecrecover compatibility. Uses keccak256 for Ethereum-style message hashing with EIP-191 prefix.

nodes.name: BridgeAttestation
nodes.summary: Cross-chain bridge attestation containing sender, recipient, amount, nonce, destination chain ID, and contract address, with Solidity-compatible hash computation for on-chain verification.

nodes.name: BridgeProof
nodes.summary: Aggregated bridge proof containing the attestation hash and collected validator signatures, verifiable against the active validator set with 2/3 threshold requirement.

nodes.name: circuit_breaker.rs
nodes.summary: Implements a circuit breaker that monitors finality progress and liveness, halting the node if finality stalls beyond a configurable round threshold or if no new vertices arrive within a timeout period.

nodes.name: CircuitBreaker
nodes.summary: Safety monitor tracking last finalized round and vertex timestamps to detect finality stalls and liveness failures.

nodes.name: check_finality
nodes.summary: Checks whether finality has progressed within the allowed round gap, returning an error with diagnostic info if the gap exceeds the configured maximum.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Barrel module re-exporting the circuit_breaker safety subsystem.

nodes.name: additional_coverage.rs
nodes.summary: Integration tests covering finality with 21 validators, deterministic ordering, timestamp validation, fee enforcement, halving schedule, epoch transitions, checkpoint persistence, and equivocation performance.

nodes.name: address_tests.rs
nodes.summary: Unit tests for address derivation from secret keys, hex/bech32m encoding round-trips, display formatting, and invalid input rejection.

nodes.name: address.rs
nodes.summary: Address format tests covering bech32m encoding/decoding, address derivation from ed25519 keys, and format validation edge cases.

nodes.name: adversarial.rs
nodes.summary: Adversarial scenario tests simulating Byzantine validators, equivocation attacks, double-spend attempts, orphan flooding, timestamp manipulation, and various malicious network behaviors.

nodes.name: bft_rules.rs
nodes.summary: Tests verifying BFT consensus rules including 2f+1 quorum requirements, single-vertex-per-round enforcement, parent validation, and finality conditions under various validator configurations.

nodes.name: block_tests.rs
nodes.summary: Tests for block construction, serialization, merkle root computation, coinbase transaction validation, and block header integrity checks.

nodes.name: bridge_solidity_compat.rs
nodes.summary: Cross-implementation compatibility tests ensuring Rust bridge signing code produces byte-identical output to the Solidity UDAGBridgeValidator contract, using golden vector assertions for EIP-191 message hashes and secp256k1 signatures.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses RFC 6979 deterministic ECDSA via k256 for reproducible golden vectors across CI runs.

nodes.name: bucket_addresses.rs
nodes.summary: Regression tests ensuring the four protocol address constants (founder, IDO, ecosystem, reserve) match their canonical bech32m representations from the April 2026 tokenomics update.

nodes.name: checkpoint_chain_test.rs
nodes.summary: Tests for checkpoint chain continuity, verifying that checkpoints form a valid hash chain with correct state roots and cosigner sets across multiple finalized rounds.

nodes.name: checkpoint_cosigning.rs
nodes.summary: Tests for checkpoint cosigning protocol including signature aggregation, threshold validation, cosigner set verification, and rejection of invalid or duplicate cosignatures.

nodes.name: checkpoint_integration.rs
nodes.summary: End-to-end checkpoint integration tests covering the full flow from DAG finalization through checkpoint creation, cosigning, persistence, and state recovery.

nodes.name: checkpoint_tests.rs
nodes.summary: Unit and integration tests for checkpoint creation, serialization, hash computation, and validation of checkpoint metadata including round numbers and state root hashes.

nodes.name: checkpoint.rs
nodes.summary: Core checkpoint tests validating checkpoint struct construction, hash stability, round-trip serialization, and basic checkpoint chain properties.

nodes.name: crash_recovery.rs
nodes.summary: Crash recovery tests exercising the full save-crash-reload cycle using real redb persistence, ensuring state roots and checkpoint hashes match after simulated node restarts.

nodes.name: cross_batch_equivocation.rs
nodes.summary: Tests detecting equivocation across finalized batches, verifying that conflicting vertices from the same validator in different finalization batches are caught and penalized.

nodes.name: crypto_correctness.rs
nodes.summary: Comprehensive cryptographic correctness tests covering ed25519 signing/verification, key derivation, signature malleability, hash function properties, and constant-time comparison guarantees.

nodes.name: dag_bft_finality.rs
nodes.summary: Tests for DAG-based BFT finality algorithm verifying correct finalization of vertices under various quorum conditions, validator set sizes, and round progression scenarios.

nodes.name: dag_structure.rs
nodes.summary: Tests for DAG structural integrity including vertex insertion, parent-child relationships, round organization, topological ordering, and pruning behavior.

nodes.name: dag_sync.rs
nodes.summary: Tests for DAG synchronization protocol verifying correct exchange of missing vertices between peers, orphan handling, and catch-up from checkpoint state.

nodes.name: double_spend_prevention.rs
nodes.summary: Tests verifying double-spend prevention through nonce tracking, conflicting transaction detection in the mempool, and correct rejection of duplicate spends across finalized batches.

nodes.name: economics_audit.rs
nodes.summary: Economic audit tests targeting bugs #170-175 from March 2026, covering reward distribution correctness, delegation edge cases, halving schedule accuracy, and supply invariant stress tests under the 7-bucket tokenomics model.

nodes.name: edge_case_coverage.rs
nodes.summary: Extensive edge case coverage with 56 test functions exercising boundary conditions across transactions, staking, governance, DAG operations, and state transitions to catch regressions in corner cases.

nodes.name: edge_cases.rs
nodes.summary: Edge case tests covering unusual but valid states including empty blocks, maximum value transfers, zero-balance accounts, and state transitions at protocol boundary conditions.

nodes.name: epoch_transition.rs
nodes.summary: Tests for epoch transition logic including active validator set rotation, stake-weighted ranking, and correct state updates when crossing epoch boundaries.

nodes.name: equivocation_evidence.rs
nodes.summary: Tests for equivocation evidence construction, verification, and slashing enforcement when validators produce conflicting vertices in the same round.

nodes.name: equivocation_gossip.rs
nodes.summary: Tests for equivocation evidence gossip protocol ensuring that evidence is propagated to all peers and that duplicate evidence is handled idempotently.

nodes.name: error_handling_tests.rs
nodes.summary: Tests verifying correct error types and messages for invalid transactions, malformed blocks, insufficient balances, and other failure modes across the state engine.

nodes.name: fault_tolerance.rs
nodes.summary: Fault tolerance tests verifying consensus progress under f Byzantine failures, network partitions, and validator crashes with correct recovery after reconnection.

nodes.name: finality.rs
nodes.summary: Core finality tests verifying that the finality tracker correctly identifies finalized vertices based on descendant validator coverage and 2f+1 quorum thresholds.

nodes.name: genesis_hash_compute.rs
nodes.summary: Tests verifying deterministic genesis checkpoint hash computation, ensuring the genesis state with council members and initial allocations produces a stable, reproducible hash.

nodes.name: genesis_runtime_check.rs
nodes.summary: Verifies genesis state construction and checkpoint hash pinning, ensuring StateEngine::new_with_genesis produces a deterministic genesis that matches the constant and panics on drift.

nodes.name: governance_integration.rs
nodes.summary: Comprehensive governance integration tests covering the full proposal lifecycle, quorum/approval, rejection, expiration, double-vote prevention, parameter change execution, DAO hibernation, and Council of 21 membership management (add/remove members, category capacity, emission eligibility, self-nomination).
nodes.languageNotes: 35 test functions with shared helper factories for proposal/vote transactions and council seating, exercising the full Council of 21 governance model.

nodes.name: governance_tests.rs
nodes.summary: Unit-level governance tests for proposal creation, voting period mechanics, proposal type variants, status transitions, vote counting, quorum calculation, and proposal ID uniqueness.

nodes.name: governance.rs
nodes.summary: Tests governance transaction serialization and signing correctness, verifying proposal and vote transaction hashes and signatures are valid and that different proposal types produce distinct hashes.

nodes.name: multi_validator_progression.rs
nodes.summary: Tests multi-validator DAG progression with 4 validators over 5 rounds, verifying complete round progression, deterministic vertex ordering, and state correctness with interleaved transactions including transfers and staking.

nodes.name: name_registry_pockets.rs
nodes.summary: Tests the name pockets feature (sub-wallets like @alice.savings), covering creation, removal, duplicate rejection, max pocket limit, invalid label validation, parent delegation, deterministic address derivation, and missing-account rejection.

nodes.name: name_registry.rs
nodes.summary: Tests the on-chain name registry system including registration, duplicate rejection, invalid name validation, tiered pricing, renewal, transfer, non-owner transfer prevention, expiry release, free perpetual names, profile updates, one-name-per-address enforcement, and treasury fee collection.

nodes.name: ordering.rs
nodes.summary: Tests deterministic topological ordering of DAG vertices, verifying identical independent orderings, stability, causal order respect, total coverage (every vertex once), strict extension property, and empty/single vertex edge cases.

nodes.name: parent_finality_guarantee.rs
nodes.summary: Tests the parent finality guarantee invariant ensuring that parent vertices are always finalized before their children in the DAG ordering.

nodes.name: parent_finality_simple.rs
nodes.summary: Simplified parent finality test verifying that a parent vertex must be finalized before its child in a basic DAG topology.

nodes.name: partial_parent_selection.rs
nodes.summary: Tests partial parent selection (k-of-n parents) for DAG scalability, verifying correct count limits, determinism, proposer-dependent variation, finality with partial parents, graph connectivity, removal of the 64-validator ceiling, and parent diversity.

nodes.name: performance.rs
nodes.summary: Performance benchmarks for finality checking at scale, measuring throughput for 1,000 and 10,000 vertex DAGs to ensure finality checks remain fast.

nodes.name: persistence_edge_cases_tests.rs
nodes.summary: Tests persistence edge cases for DAG save/load, existence checks, nonexistent loads, empty DAGs, multi-vertex persistence, checkpoint save/load, and mempool persistence with roundtrip consistency verification.

nodes.name: phantom_validator.rs
nodes.summary: Tests phantom validator handling where a validator disappears from the active set, verifying that configured_validator_count prevents a missing validator from breaking finality, and that without it finality stalls.

nodes.name: pruning.rs
nodes.summary: Comprehensive DAG pruning tests covering horizon-based removal, unfinalized vertex protection, pruning floor persistence, archive mode bypass, custom depth, finality preservation after pruning, children/equivocation/descendant_validators cleanup, memory stats reporting, and memory leak verification.

nodes.name: recovery.rs

nodes.name: corrupted_finality_bin_handled_gracefully
nodes.summary: Tests graceful handling of corrupted finality.bin file, verifying the node detects the corruption and recovers.

nodes.name: all_state_files_corrupted_node_recovers
nodes.summary: Tests the worst-case scenario where all state files (redb, dag.bin, finality.bin) are corrupted simultaneously, verifying the node can still recover.

nodes.name: cross_feature.rs
nodes.summary: Tests that staking, delegation, governance, and bridge features converge correctly when exercised simultaneously, including under message reordering.

nodes.name: delegation.rs
nodes.summary: Simulation tests for delegated staking rewards convergence across validators, including under message reordering conditions.

nodes.name: determinism.rs
nodes.summary: Verifies deterministic state convergence by running identical configurations with different message orderings and seeds, asserting all validators reach the same state root.

nodes.name: e2e.rs
nodes.summary: Comprehensive end-to-end test suite covering the complete system: validator lifecycle, delegation lifecycle, governance lifecycle, transfer flow, multi-validator consensus, checkpoint lifecycle, and network partition recovery.
nodes.languageNotes: Directly instantiates BlockDag, FinalityTracker, and StateEngine rather than using the SimHarness, providing lower-level end-to-end verification of the full consensus + state + governance stack.

nodes.name: e2e_validator_lifecycle
nodes.summary: Tests complete validator lifecycle: genesis, stake, produce blocks, earn rewards, unstake, withdraw.

nodes.name: e2e_delegation_lifecycle
nodes.summary: Tests complete delegation lifecycle: delegate to validator, earn proportional rewards, undelegate, withdraw.

nodes.name: e2e_governance_lifecycle
nodes.summary: Tests complete governance lifecycle: council proposal creation, voting, quorum achievement, execution delay, and parameter change application.

nodes.name: e2e_network_partition_recovery
nodes.summary: Tests network partition and recovery: validators split into partitions, produce conflicting blocks, then heal and converge on a single canonical state.

nodes.name: epoch.rs
nodes.summary: Tests that epoch transitions correctly update the active validator set and that all validators converge on the same active set after transition.

nodes.name: equivocation.rs
nodes.summary: Tests that equivocating validators are detected and slashed, and that the supply invariant holds even when equivocators inject conflicting transactions.

nodes.name: exhaustive_properties.rs
nodes.summary: Exhaustive property-based tests verifying protocol invariants (supply bounds, active set convergence) hold across staking, delegation, governance, equivocation, and high validator counts.

nodes.name: finality_attack.rs
nodes.summary: Tests that finality-stalling attackers cannot halt the network when honest validators hold supermajority, including scenarios with two concurrent stallers within BFT tolerance.

nodes.name: fuzz_adversarial.proptest-regressions
nodes.summary: Proptest regression seeds for adversarial fuzz tests, recording 2 previously-failing inputs involving stake+stale-tx and finality-stall attack sequences.

nodes.name: fuzz_adversarial.rs
nodes.summary: Property-based fuzz tests using proptest to generate random Byzantine attack plans (equivocation, stale txs, finality stalling) and verify protocol invariants hold across varying validator counts and network conditions.

nodes.name: fuzz_extended.rs
nodes.summary: Extended cross-feature fuzz tests using proptest to generate random combinations of staking, delegation, governance, and bridge operations alongside Byzantine actions, verifying invariant convergence.

nodes.name: governance_attack.rs
nodes.summary: Tests governance attack resistance: verifying that hostile takeover fails without quorum and that extreme parameter values are bounded by protocol limits.

nodes.name: governance_full.rs
nodes.summary: Comprehensive governance test suite with 17 tests covering all proposal types, lifecycle stages, weighted voting, quorum requirements, approval thresholds, execution delays, council management, treasury spending, BFT safety bounds, and behavior under network partitions, reordering, packet loss, and latency.

nodes.name: governance.rs
nodes.summary: Basic governance simulation tests verifying parameter change proposals converge across validators, including under message reordering.

nodes.name: long_running.rs
nodes.summary: Long-running stress tests: 10,000-round stability, latency stress, combined Byzantine attacks over extended periods, cross-feature lifecycle, and memory-bounded pruning verification.

nodes.name: p2p_consensus.rs
nodes.summary: P2P integration tests verifying that 3 real nodes reach consensus and agree on total supply when communicating over TCP.

nodes.name: three_nodes_reach_consensus
nodes.summary: Spawns 3 real nodes and verifies they reach consensus over the actual P2P network layer.

nodes.name: nodes_agree_on_supply
nodes.summary: Verifies all nodes report identical total supply after reaching consensus, ensuring no supply divergence.

nodes.name: p2p_flooding.rs
nodes.summary: P2P flood resistance tests verifying the node handles many concurrent connections and rapid connection churn without crashing or leaking resources.

nodes.name: many_concurrent_connections
nodes.summary: Opens many simultaneous TCP connections to a node to test connection handling limits and resource cleanup.

nodes.name: p2p_handshake.rs
nodes.summary: P2P handshake security tests verifying that random bytes, invalid Noise key material, and handshake floods are all handled gracefully without crashing the node.

nodes.name: random_bytes_during_handshake
nodes.summary: Sends random garbage bytes during the Noise handshake to verify the node rejects malformed connections gracefully.

nodes.name: handshake_flood
nodes.summary: Rapidly initiates many concurrent handshakes to test the node's resistance to handshake-based denial of service.

nodes.name: p2p_malformed.rs
nodes.summary: Integration tests verifying that real nodes gracefully handle malformed P2P input: garbage bytes before Noise handshake, oversized length prefixes, rapid connect/disconnect, and incomplete handshakes.

nodes.name: partition.rs
nodes.summary: Simulation tests for network partition scenarios: a 3-vs-1 split where the majority group finalizes and the minority catches up after healing, and a 2-vs-2 split where neither group can finalize.

nodes.name: reorder.rs
nodes.summary: Simulation tests verifying DAG consensus converges when messages arrive in random order, including a multi-seed sweep across 100 seeds for statistical confidence.

nodes.name: reward_attack.rs
nodes.summary: Byzantine simulation tests proving a RewardGambler attacker cannot inflate the token supply, run under both perfect delivery and random message reordering.

nodes.name: rpc_fuzz.rs
nodes.summary: Comprehensive RPC endpoint fuzz test that sends malformed JSON, garbage bytes, oversized payloads, wrong HTTP methods, and path traversal attempts to every UltraDAG RPC endpoint, asserting no 500 errors or crashes.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses raw TCP streams instead of an HTTP client library, avoiding external dependencies while testing HTTP edge cases including CORS preflight and binary payloads.

nodes.name: sdk_parity.rs
nodes.summary: Cross-SDK signable_bytes parity tests verifying the canonical Rust byte layout for all 7 transaction types (Transfer, Stake, Unstake, Delegate, Undelegate, SetCommission, Vote) matches the expected format used by JS, Python, and Go SDKs.

nodes.name: selective_equivocation.rs
nodes.summary: Byzantine simulation tests for selective equivocation attacks, verifying state convergence and that equivocation evidence is detected under both perfect and reordered delivery.

nodes.name: staking.rs
nodes.summary: Simulation tests for the full staking lifecycle (stake, earn rewards, unstake) verifying the supply invariant holds under both perfect delivery and random message reordering.

nodes.name: stress.rs
nodes.summary: Stress tests running 21 validators with heavy transaction load and 5% message loss, plus mixed Byzantine scenarios (equivocator + crash) within BFT tolerance.

nodes.name: tx_flooding.rs
nodes.summary: Regression tests for Bug #174 (stale-nonce transaction flooding) verifying the saturating fee clawback fix prevents supply invariant violations and network halts, under both perfect and reordered delivery.

nodes.name: rpc_fuzz_malformed_inputs
nodes.summary: Main fuzz test function exercising every RPC endpoint (tx, faucet, stake, unstake, delegate, undelegate, set-commission, proposal, vote, balance, round, vertex, CORS preflight) with malformed inputs and verifying graceful error handling.

nodes.name: http_post
nodes.summary: Raw TCP HTTP POST helper that sends a request and parses the status code, avoiding external HTTP client dependencies.

nodes.name: canonical_transfer_signable_bytes
nodes.summary: Verifies the Transfer transaction signable_bytes canonical layout (91 bytes: NETWORK_ID + tag + from + to + amount + fee + nonce) and round-trips the signature.

nodes.name: stale_nonce_flooding_no_longer_halts
nodes.summary: Regression test proving a DuplicateTxFlooder Byzantine attacker can no longer halt the network via stale-nonce transactions, verifying honest validators finalize past round 200.

nodes.name: partition_and_heal
nodes.summary: Tests a 3-vs-1 network partition where the majority group maintains BFT quorum and finalizes, then verifies the minority validator converges after partition healing.

nodes.name: random_order_multi_seed
nodes.summary: Runs the random message reorder scenario across 100 different seeds to provide statistical confidence in convergence under arbitrary delivery ordering.

nodes.name: build.rs
nodes.summary: Cargo build script that invokes embuild to generate ESP-IDF linker configuration and kconfig bindings for the ESP32 target.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the ESP32 light client crate, configuring esp-idf-svc, ed25519-dalek crypto, blake3 hashing, optional SSD1306 OLED display, and size-optimized release profiles targeting 4 MB flash.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive guide for the ESP32 hardware wallet / light client covering what the device is (and isn't), wallet security with eFuse flash encryption, prerequisites, build/flash instructions, serial output expectations, flash/RAM footprint analysis, and troubleshooting.

nodes.name: rust-toolchain.toml
nodes.summary: Pins the Rust toolchain to the Espressif esp channel required for Xtensa target compilation on ESP32.

nodes.name: sdkconfig.defaults
nodes.summary: ESP-IDF kconfig overrides tuning stack size (16 KB), enabling mbedTLS root certificate bundle for HTTPS, disabling Bluetooth to save flash, and configuring lwIP socket limits for the ESP32 light client.
nodes.languageNotes: ESP-IDF sdkconfig format uses CONFIG_* keys; these override defaults at build time via the CMake/embuild pipeline.

nodes.name: client.rs
nodes.summary: HTTP RPC client for talking to an UltraDAG node from the ESP32, providing typed wrappers around GET /status, GET /balance, GET /validators, POST /faucet, and POST /tx/submit with raw esp-idf-svc HTTP stack (no reqwest).

nodes.name: UltraDagClient
nodes.summary: HTTP RPC client struct wrapping a base URL, with methods for status polling, balance queries, faucet requests, validator listing, and transaction submission via raw ESP-IDF HTTP client.

nodes.name: config.rs
nodes.summary: Compile-time firmware configuration constants for WiFi credentials, UltraDAG node RPC URL, watch addresses to monitor, and poll interval -- all baked into .rodata at flash time.

nodes.name: display.rs
nodes.summary: Optional SSD1306 OLED display driver (128x32 or 128x64) over I2C, providing a show_lines method to render up to 4 lines of status text. Fails silently if no display is wired, allowing headless operation.

nodes.name: OledDisplay
nodes.summary: Wrapper around an SSD1306 OLED display with I2C initialization and multi-line text rendering using embedded-graphics MonoTextStyle.

nodes.name: finality.rs
nodes.summary: Trust-on-first-use (TOFU) validator-set verification: on first boot snapshots the validator address set as a blake3 Merkle root in NVS, then on subsequent boots detects if the validator set changed -- a defense-in-depth proxy until the node exposes a signed checkpoint endpoint.
nodes.languageNotes: TOFU approach is an interim measure; full cryptographic light-client verification requires a /checkpoint RPC endpoint that the node does not yet expose.

nodes.name: main.rs
nodes.summary: Entry point for the ESP32 light client: boots ESP-IDF, connects to WiFi, loads or creates an Ed25519 wallet from NVS, runs a once-per-boot wallet flow (faucet funding, demo transfer via legacy or SmartTransfer), then enters an infinite polling loop querying node status, balances, and validator-set TOFU checks with LED and OLED feedback.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Application entry point that initializes ESP-IDF, connects WiFi, loads wallet keys, runs the demo wallet flow, and enters the infinite status-polling loop with LED blink and OLED display updates.

nodes.name: sign.rs
nodes.summary: On-chip Ed25519 transaction signing for three transaction types: SignedTransfer (legacy), SignedAddKey (SmartAccount bootstrap), and SignedSmartTransfer (multi-key capable). Each struct builds a deterministic signable-bytes payload byte-exact with the Rust node's format, signs with ed25519-dalek, and serializes to JSON for RPC submission.
nodes.languageNotes: Signable-bytes construction must be byte-exact with the main ultradag-coin node implementation; any drift causes signature verification failures.

nodes.name: SignedTransfer
nodes.summary: Legacy transfer transaction builder that constructs signable bytes (tag + from + to + amount + fee + nonce), signs with Ed25519, and serializes to JSON envelope for /tx/submit.

nodes.name: SignedAddKey
nodes.summary: AddKey transaction builder for SmartAccount bootstrap, registering a secondary Ed25519 key via deterministic derivation from the primary seed.

nodes.name: SignedSmartTransfer
nodes.summary: SmartTransfer transaction builder using key_id-based signing for multi-key SmartAccounts, the modern transfer path matching the phone wallet's code path.

nodes.name: storage.rs
nodes.summary: NVS (Non-Volatile Storage) abstraction for persisting wallet seed, WiFi credentials, and TOFU validator-set root hash across reboots on the ESP32's flash partition.

nodes.name: Storage
nodes.summary: NVS wrapper providing typed get/set for wallet seed (auto-generated on first boot), WiFi SSID/password, and validator Merkle root persistence.

nodes.name: wallet.rs
nodes.summary: Wallet key management: loads an Ed25519 signing key from NVS or generates a new one on first boot, derives the 20-byte address from the public key via blake3 hash truncation.

nodes.name: load_or_create
nodes.summary: Loads an Ed25519 signing key from NVS storage, or generates a random 32-byte seed and persists it on first boot, returning the derived SigningKey.

nodes.name: wifi.rs
nodes.summary: WiFi station-mode connection helper that initializes the ESP32 radio, associates with a WPA2-PSK access point, waits for DHCP lease, and returns the blocking WiFi handle that must be kept alive for the program's lifetime.

nodes.name: index.css
nodes.summary: Root stylesheet defining UltraDAG design system CSS custom properties (colors, fonts, spacing), dark/light theme tokens, responsive breakpoints, and base element styles.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines 15 CSS custom properties for the DAG color palette with automatic dark/light theme switching via data-theme attribute.

nodes.name: api.ts
nodes.summary: Central API client module providing all RPC calls to UltraDAG nodes (status, balances, staking, governance, bridge, names, streams), plus bech32m address encoding/decoding and formatting utilities.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains a full bech32m implementation for UltraDAG native addresses alongside 40+ typed RPC endpoint wrappers.

nodes.name: connectToNode
nodes.summary: Races multiple testnet/mainnet node URLs to find the fastest responding node and sets it as the active connection.

nodes.name: addressToBech32
nodes.summary: Encodes a hex address to bech32m format with the udag/udagt human-readable prefix.

nodes.name: bech32ToHex
nodes.summary: Decodes a bech32m address string back to its hex representation with checksum verification.

nodes.name: formatProposalType
nodes.summary: Converts a governance proposal type object into a human-readable string description for dashboard display.

nodes.name: badges.ts
nodes.summary: Badge computation engine that evaluates on-chain profile data (balance, stake, delegation, proposals, validator status, GitHub contributions) to award achievement badges with icons and levels.

nodes.name: computeBadges
nodes.summary: Evaluates a BadgeInput against multiple achievement criteria (hodler tiers, staking, delegation, governance participation, validator status, GitHub bounties) and returns earned badges.

nodes.name: contracts.ts
nodes.summary: EVM contract ABIs and addresses for the UDAG bridge on Arbitrum, including UDAGToken ERC-20 and UDAGBridge interfaces with placeholder deployment addresses.

nodes.name: github.ts
nodes.summary: GitHub bounty system client that fetches issues from the UltraDAG repo, parses bounty frontmatter (UDAG rewards, severity, status), and categorizes them for the bounty board UI.

nodes.name: fetchBountyIssues
nodes.summary: Fetches open GitHub issues labeled as bounties, filters and parses them into structured ParsedBounty objects with reward amounts and categories.

nodes.name: keygen.ts
nodes.summary: Ed25519 keypair generation using the noble-ed25519 library, deriving UltraDAG addresses from public keys via SHA-256 hashing.

nodes.name: keystore.ts
nodes.summary: Encrypted wallet keystore using AES-GCM with PBKDF2 key derivation, supporting multiple wallet slots, password-based lock/unlock, WebAuthn biometric enrollment for passwordless access, and import/export.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Web Crypto API (SubtleCrypto) for AES-GCM encryption and PBKDF2 key derivation, plus WebAuthn PRF extension for biometric-derived encryption keys.

nodes.name: enrollWebAuthn
nodes.summary: Enrolls a WebAuthn credential with PRF extension to derive an encryption key from biometric authentication, enabling passwordless keystore unlock.

nodes.name: unlockWithWebAuthn
nodes.summary: Unlocks the keystore using a stored WebAuthn credential and PRF-derived encryption key, falling back to credential ID matching.

nodes.name: mnemonic.ts
nodes.summary: BIP-39 mnemonic phrase generation and validation using the scure-bip39 library, with seed-to-Ed25519-keypair derivation for UltraDAG wallet recovery.

nodes.name: names.ts
nodes.summary: Name resolution library for UltraDAG's naming system, handling pocket sub-addresses (@alice.savings) with validation, parsing, and RPC-based resolution.

nodes.name: resolvePocket
nodes.summary: Resolves a pocket name (e.g., @alice.savings) to an address by querying the smart account's profile for matching pocket labels.

nodes.name: notifications.ts
nodes.summary: Browser notification abstraction layer managing permissions, enabled state persistence, and balance-change detection to push native desktop/mobile notifications.

nodes.name: passkey-signin.ts
nodes.summary: WebAuthn-based sign-in flow that authenticates users via passkeys, decompresses P256 public keys, verifies credentials against on-chain smart accounts, and derives UltraDAG addresses.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements P-256 point decompression (modPow over secp256r1 field) and SPKI encoding in pure TypeScript for WebAuthn credential verification.

nodes.name: decompressP256
nodes.summary: Decompresses a 33-byte compressed P-256 public key to 65-byte uncompressed form using big-integer modular exponentiation over the secp256r1 prime field.

nodes.name: passkey-wallet.ts
nodes.summary: Passkey wallet state manager that stores P256 credential data in localStorage, derives UltraDAG addresses from passkey public keys, and manages biometric unlock lifecycle.

nodes.name: unlockWithBiometric
nodes.summary: Performs a WebAuthn assertion to biometrically unlock the passkey wallet, verifying the credential matches the stored one.

nodes.name: theme.ts
nodes.summary: Design system constants exporting color tokens, font families, spacing values, and reusable inline-style objects (card, sidebar, button) that reference CSS custom properties for theme-aware rendering.

nodes.name: webauthn-sign.ts
nodes.summary: WebAuthn transaction signing module that serializes UltraDAG smart-account operations into binary format, signs them via passkey assertion, and assembles the signed transaction envelope for submission.
nodes.languageNotes: Manual binary serialization of transaction operations using little-endian encoding and DER-to-raw signature conversion for P-256 WebAuthn assertions.

nodes.name: serializeOperation
nodes.summary: Serializes a smart-account operation (transfer, stake, delegate, name registration, etc.) into a binary byte array matching the on-chain expected format.

nodes.name: derToRaw
nodes.summary: Converts a DER-encoded ECDSA signature from WebAuthn into a 64-byte raw (r || s) format for on-chain verification.

nodes.name: world-outline.ts
nodes.summary: Static geographic coordinate data containing simplified world coastline polygons (Natural Earth 110m) for rendering a world map outline on the dashboard canvas.

nodes.name: handleBridgeToArbitrum

nodes.languageNotes: Uses compile-time assertions and cfg(not(feature = mainnet)) gating for testnet-only constants like faucet seeds.

nodes.name: error.rs
nodes.summary: Comprehensive error enum (CoinError) covering all protocol error conditions: transaction validation, balance checks, protocol invariant violations, governance rule violations, staking/delegation errors, and bridge operation failures.

nodes.languageNotes: Uses ed25519_dalek::SigningKey::generate with OsRng directly, then imports through SecretKey::from_bytes to guarantee byte-identical addresses with mainnet nodes.

nodes.languageNotes: Models both honest and Byzantine validators; honest DAGs enforce one-vertex-per-(validator,round) matching try_insert() in dag.rs. Equivocating vertices exist globally but are filtered from honest parent sets.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand what UltraDAG is: a DAG-BFT consensus protocol designed for permissioned networks and IoT applications. Instead of a linear blockchain, validators produce vertices in a directed acyclic graph, enabling parallel block production with deterministic finality. The README introduces the key primitives (Ed25519 signatures, BLAKE3 hashing, 21M supply cap, 7-bucket emission) and links to quickstart, architecture docs, and the whitepaper.

tour.title: Node Entry Point
tour.description: The main.rs binary is where a validator or full node boots. It parses CLI flags via clap, loads persisted state from redb, discovers seed peers, performs fast-sync if needed, then launches three concurrent subsystems: the validator loop (vertex production), the RPC server (HTTP API), and an optional TUI dashboard. This single file shows how every layer of the stack gets wired together at startup.
tour.languageLesson: Rust's #[tokio::main] attribute macro transforms main() into an async runtime entry point. The clap derive macro generates CLI argument parsing from struct field annotations, eliminating manual argument handling code.

tour.title: Core Protocol Library
tour.description: The ultradag-coin crate is the heart of the project, re-exporting every protocol module: consensus, state, transactions, governance, bridge, and constants. Understanding this crate's module tree is essential because every other crate (node, network, simulator) depends on it. The constants file defines all protocol parameters: 21M UDAG supply cap, emission percentages per bucket, minimum stake, epoch length, and governance thresholds.

tour.title: DAG Structure and Vertices
tour.description: A DagVertex is the fundamental unit of consensus: it contains a block payload (transactions), parent hashes linking to previous vertices, a round number, and the producing validator's Ed25519 signature. The BlockDag data structure stores all vertices and manages insertion with validation (signature checks, parent existence, equivocation detection). This is the 'directed acyclic graph' that replaces a linear chain, allowing multiple validators to produce vertices in parallel within the same round.
tour.languageLesson: Rust's ownership system ensures that once a DagVertex is inserted into the BlockDag, it cannot be mutated. The DAG uses HashMap<[u8;32], DagVertex> where the key is the BLAKE3 hash, giving O(1) vertex lookups by hash.

tour.title: Finality and Ordering
tour.description: The FinalityTracker determines when vertices achieve BFT finality by checking whether 2f+1 distinct validators have produced descendant vertices (the 'descendant-coverage' rule). Once finalized, vertices are deterministically ordered by (round, hash) for consistent transaction execution across all nodes. This two-step process -- finality detection then deterministic ordering -- is what gives UltraDAG its 2-round finality guarantee while maintaining a total order for state transitions.
tour.languageLesson: BitVec replaces HashSet<Address> for tracking descendant validators, achieving 256x memory reduction at scale. Each bit position maps to a validator index, so checking 2f+1 coverage becomes a popcount operation.

tour.title: Validator Set and Epochs
tour.description: The ValidatorSet manages registration, allowlist filtering, and BFT quorum calculation (2f+1 threshold). Epochs synchronize validator set changes: the epoch module reads staked validators from the state engine and updates the finality tracker at epoch boundaries. This separation ensures that stake changes mid-epoch do not destabilize consensus -- new validators only become active at the next epoch transition.

tour.title: State Engine
tour.description: The StateEngine is the state machine at the core of UltraDAG. It holds all accounts, staking positions, delegations, governance proposals, council membership, and supply tracking. When finalized vertices are ordered (Step 5), their transactions are applied to this engine sequentially. The engine enforces the supply invariant (liquid + staked + delegated + treasury == total_supply) and persists to redb for crash recovery. Every economic rule in UltraDAG flows through this single struct.
tour.languageLesson: Rust's redb (an embedded ACID database) replaces JSON snapshots and write-ahead logs. Tables are defined with compile-time key/value type safety, and all writes within a transaction are atomic -- the engine either persists completely or not at all.

tour.title: Transaction System
tour.description: The Transaction enum defines all 27 operation types the protocol supports: basic transfers, staking/unstaking, delegation, governance votes, bridge deposits/releases, smart account operations (WebAuthn passkey wallets), name registry, and streaming payments. Each variant carries its own validation rules and state transition logic. The smart account subsystem is particularly notable -- it enables passkey-native P256/WebAuthn wallets with multi-key management, social recovery, and spending policies, bridging Web2 authentication into a blockchain context.

tour.title: Governance: Council of 21
tour.description: Governance is not stake-weighted: it runs through a Council of 21 members across seven seat categories (Engineering, Community, Growth, Operations, Legal, Research, Security). Each council member gets exactly one vote, and only council members can create and vote on proposals. Proposal types include parameter changes, treasury spends, emergency actions, and council membership management. The snapshot_total_stake field (actually council size) at proposal creation prevents quorum manipulation.

tour.title: P2P Networking
tour.description: The networking layer handles peer discovery, encrypted communication, and DAG vertex gossip. Peers authenticate via a Noise XX handshake (X25519/ChaChaPoly/BLAKE2s) with Ed25519 identity binding. The P2P message protocol covers vertex proposals, transaction gossip, checkpoint sync, and equivocation evidence propagation. The PeerRegistry tracks active connections with ban lists and TOCTOU-safe connection state, while the NodeServer orchestrates the gossip loop with orphan buffer management and per-peer caps.

tour.title: Validator Loop and RPC
tour.description: The validator loop runs each round: it checks active-set membership, pulls transactions from the mempool, creates a new DagVertex with parent references, signs it, and gossips it to peers. The RPC server exposes the full node API over HTTP -- balance queries, transaction submission, staking, delegation, governance, DAG inspection, and monitoring endpoints. Together, the validator loop and RPC are the two primary runtime threads that keep a node producing blocks and serving clients.

tour.title: Simulation and Formal Verification
tour.description: The simulation harness orchestrates multi-validator test runs with configurable network conditions, byzantine strategies (equivocation, withholding, timestamp manipulation), and safety invariant checking. Invariant checkers verify state convergence, supply conservation, finality monotonicity, and stake consistency after every round. Beyond simulation, a TLA+ formal specification models the leaderless DAG-BFT consensus, and the model checker verifies safety and liveness properties across bounded state spaces.
tour.languageLesson: TLA+ is a formal specification language where you declare state variables, define initial conditions and next-state relations, and then assert temporal properties (invariants, liveness). The TLC model checker exhaustively explores all reachable states to verify these properties hold.

tour.title: Bridge to Arbitrum
tour.description: The bridge enables cross-chain transfers between UltraDAG's native chain and Arbitrum (Ethereum L2). On the Solidity side, UDAGToken is an ERC-20 with 8 decimals, 21M cap, and role-based access control, while UDAGBridgeValidator implements a validator-federation model requiring 2/3+ threshold signatures for withdrawal claims. On the Rust side, BridgeAttestation construction and Ed25519 signature verification connect the native chain's state engine to the Solidity contracts. The presale-first strategy deploys the token with zero validators so the ERC-20 is tradable before the bridge activates.
tour.languageLesson: Solidity's OpenZeppelin AccessControl pattern uses role-based permissions (BRIDGE_ROLE, PAUSER_ROLE) instead of a single owner. The validator-federation bridge requires collecting off-chain signatures and submitting them in a single on-chain transaction, keeping gas costs proportional to the signature count, not the validator set size.

tour.title: Dashboard and SDKs
tour.description: The React dashboard provides a complete UI for interacting with UltraDAG: wallet management with passkey support, staking, delegation, governance voting, bridge operations, and network monitoring. Its central api.ts module wraps every RPC endpoint from Step 11. Four SDKs (Rust, JavaScript, Python, Go) give developers typed clients for building on UltraDAG, each mirroring the same RPC surface with language-idiomatic patterns -- from Rust's async/await to Python's requests-based HTTP client.

tour.title: Deployment and CI/CD
tour.description: The deployment pipeline ties everything together. The Dockerfile downloads pre-built binaries from GitHub Releases for both testnet and mainnet variants. The GitHub Actions CI pipeline builds Linux x86_64 binaries on every push to main and publishes them as a release. The testnet deploy script manages 5 Fly.io nodes with --clean (state wipe) and --restart flags, using internal WireGuard DNS for P2P communication. This infrastructure layer is what turns the Rust codebase into a running multi-node network.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions workflows define CI/CD as code: the 'on' trigger fires on push to main, 'jobs' run in parallel, and 'steps' execute sequentially within a job. Fly.io's internal .internal DNS lets nodes discover each other over WireGuard without exposing P2P ports to the public internet.
